multiplechoiceresponse.label: Several Grad-CAM overlays (e.g., for coast, harbor, and village) highlight overlapping regions of the image, particularly near the water and shoreline. Which interpretation best explains this observation?

choicegroup.choice: The model is confused and unable to distinguish between these classes.
These classes share common visual features, which the model reuses while still weighting them differently for each label.
Grad-CAM always highlights the most visually salient region of the image, regardless of class.
The model ignores class labels once the image is passed through the network.

div.p: Many scene categories share visual components, such as water, coastline, or nearby buildings. Grad-CAM reveals that the model reuses these shared visual features while combining them differently depending on the class being evaluated. Overlap in highlighted regions does not imply confusion. Instead, it shows that these classes share common visual structures, such as water, shoreline, or nearby buildings. The model reuses these shared cues but weights them differently depending on which class you ask it to explain.
